title: MediaMolder Pipeline Config
description: Schema v1.0 for MediaMolder JSON pipeline configuration.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the pipeline job.

ffmpeg_cmd.description: Equivalent FFmpeg command line (advisory only — the runtime ignores this field; auto-populated on import via convert-cmd or the GUI; refreshed on every GUI save; if you edit this JSON manually, you must also update or remove this field).

copy_ts.description: Preserve the original demuxer timestamps end-to-end instead of rebasing every input to PTS 0. Mirrors ffmpeg's global -copyts: suppresses the demuxer-side ts_offset shift that normally accompanies -ss, and changes the meaning of any output-side -ss / -to (in output.options) to absolute timeline values rather than offsets from the input's start. Required for accurate broadcast / HLS PTS handling.

start_at_zero.description: Modulates copy_ts. When true, re-enables the demuxer-side ts_offset shift even under copy_ts so the first kept packet still anchors at PTS 0 while later timing is preserved. Mirrors ffmpeg's global -start_at_zero (fftools/ffmpeg_demux.c L486). Requires copy_ts=true.

filter_complex_threads.description: Pipeline-wide cap on threads per filter graph. Mirrors ffmpeg's global -filter_complex_threads. Written to AVFilterGraph.nb_threads. 0 leaves libavfilter's default in place. Per-node `threads` overrides this.

assets.description: Named asset registry. Each key is a symbolic asset name referenced in filter params as "$asset:<name>"; the runtime substitutes the resolved filesystem path before building the libavfilter graph. Typical use-cases: fonts for drawtext=/subtitles=, ML models for arnndn=, LUT files for lut3d=/haldclut=.

hardware_devices.description: Named hardware-acceleration device contexts. Each entry is opened via av_hwdevice_ctx_create at pipeline start and referenced from nodes by name via the "device" field. Mirrors ffmpeg's -init_hw_device. (Wave 10 #56)

filter_asset_paths.description: Directories searched when resolving relative model-file paths that appear directly in filter params (e.g. arnndn model=rnnoise.rnnn, sofalizer sofa=file.sofa). Checked in declaration order after the pipeline file's own directory. Absolute param values are checked directly; $asset: references bypass this check. (Wave 11 #66)

kind.description: How to open the input. "file" (default) probes the URL with libavformat. "lavfi" routes through the lavfi virtual demuxer. "raw" forces a rawvideo / PCM demuxer (requires format + geometry/audio params). "concat" opens an inline concat_list (or an existing listfile pointed to by url) via libavformat's concat demuxer.

format.description: Force libavformat demuxer name (mirrors ffmpeg `-f FMT`). Required when kind="raw". Common values: rawvideo, s16le, image2, mpegts, concat.

framerate.description: Frame rate for unframed/raw video and image2 sequences (mirrors `-framerate`). Pushed to the demuxer as `framerate`.

pixel_format.description: Planar layout of unframed/raw video frames (mirrors `-pix_fmt`). Required when kind="raw" + format=rawvideo.

video_size.description: Frame size of unframed/raw video as WxH or a libavutil named preset (hd720/vga/ntsc/...). Mirrors `-video_size` / `-s`.

sample_rate.description: Sample rate (Hz) of unframed/raw PCM audio (mirrors `-ar`). Required when kind="raw" + format names a PCM demuxer.

channels.description: Channel count of unframed/raw PCM audio (mirrors `-ac`). Required when kind="raw" + format names a PCM demuxer.

sample_fmt.description: Optional libavutil sample format for audio raw demuxers that accept it (mirrors input-side `-sample_fmt`).

concat_list.description: In-config concat playlist used when kind="concat". Each entry is one row of libavformat's concat-demuxer listfile grammar; the runtime serialises into a temp file at open time.

accurate_seek.description: Toggle accurate (decode-and-discard until target PTS) vs fast (snap-to-keyframe) seeking when -ss is set. Mirrors `-accurate_seek` / `-noaccurate_seek`. Default true.

seek_timestamp.description: When true, interpret `-ss` as an absolute container timestamp rather than an offset from start_time (mirrors `-seek_timestamp 1`).

thread_queue_size.description: Demuxer input packet queue depth in frames (mirrors `-thread_queue_size`).

protocol_whitelist.description: Restrict which libavformat protocols this input may dereference (mirrors `-protocol_whitelist`). Joined with commas before being pushed to the demuxer.

pattern_type.description: Image-sequence matcher used by the image2 demuxer (mirrors `-pattern_type`). Empty = libavformat default ("glob_sequence" autodetect).

subtitle_charenc.description: Source character encoding for text-subtitle decoders (mirrors per-input `-sub_charenc`). Rejected at decoder-open when applied to a bitmap-subtitle stream.

map_metadata.description: When true, copy this input's container-level metadata onto every output that does not set its own `metadata`. Mirrors ffmpeg `-map_metadata IDX`. Multiple inputs merge in declaration order; last writer wins per key.

map_chapters.description: When true, copy this input's chapter table onto every output that does not set its own `chapters`. Mirrors ffmpeg `-map_chapters IDX`. First input with map_chapters=true wins (single-source semantics).

stream_loop.description: Number of additional times the demuxer rewinds to the start and re-emits packets after EOF. 0 = no loop, N>0 = play N+1 times total, -1 = infinite. Mirrors ffmpeg `-stream_loop N`.

itsoffset.description: Per-input timestamp offset in seconds. Mirrors ffmpeg `-itsoffset T`.

read_rate.description: Pace packet reads to (read_rate × realtime). 0 = unpaced; 1.0 mirrors ffmpeg `-re`.

read_rate_initial_burst.description: Seconds of media time read unpaced at the start of the input. Defaults to 0.5s when read_rate is non-zero.

read_rate_catchup.description: Multiplier used to recover from a pacing lag; must be >= read_rate when set.

hwaccel.description: Hardware acceleration API for decoding video streams in this input. Mirrors FFmpeg's per-input -hwaccel flag (e.g. "cuda", "vaapi", "qsv", "videotoolbox"). Empty = software decode. (Wave 10 #59)

hwaccel_device.description: Name of a hardware_devices entry to use for decoding this input. Mirrors FFmpeg's per-input -hwaccel_device flag. Ignored when hwaccel is empty. (Wave 10 #59)

hwaccel_output_format.description: Pixel format produced by the hardware decoder. Mirrors FFmpeg's per-input -hwaccel_output_format flag. Software formats (nv12, yuv420p, …) trigger automatic hw→sw transfer; hardware formats (cuda, vaapi, …) keep frames on the GPU. (Wave 10 #59)

file.description: Path or URL of the segment. Quoted with single quotes in the serialised concat listfile; embedded apostrophes are rejected at validate time.

duration.description: Optional segment duration in seconds (mirrors concat-demuxer `duration` directive).

outpoint.description: Optional segment out-point in seconds. Must be > inpoint when both are set.

metadata.description: Optional key=value pairs attached to every packet emitted from this segment via AV_PKT_DATA_STRINGS_METADATA.

output_media_type.description: Declared output media type. Required for cross-media-type filters (showwavespic, showspectrumpic, showvolume, ...) where the output type cannot be inferred from the inbound edge type. Auto-filled from a curated registry when omitted.

device.description: Name of a hardware_devices entry whose opened AVHWDeviceContext is used for HW-accelerated encode/decode/filter on this node. (Wave 10 #56)

auto_map_hw.description: Opt-in per-node hardware filter auto-mapping. When true, the sw filter name (e.g. "scale") is promoted to its hw equivalent (e.g. "scale_cuda") based on the device type, and hwupload/hwdownload nodes are inserted at device boundaries. Requires "device" to be set. (Wave 10 #58)

codec_tag_video.description: FourCC override for the video stream's codec_tag (e.g. 'hvc1' for HEVC in MP4). Equivalent to ffmpeg -tag:v.

encoder_params_video.description: Codec-specific options (preset, crf, b, g, ...) attached to the implicit video encoder. Populated by compat/ffcli when parsing FFmpeg command lines.

max_frames_video.description: Stop muxing after N video packets. 0 = unlimited. Mirrors ffmpeg -frames:v / -vframes; required for extract-frame, tile-thumbnails, scene-image patterns.

max_frames_audio.description: Stop muxing after N audio packets. 0 = unlimited. Mirrors ffmpeg -frames:a / -aframes.

fps_mode.description: Per-output video frame-rate enforcement. Mirrors ffmpeg -fps_mode (and the legacy -vsync alias rewritten by compat/ffcli). passthrough/empty = no change; vfr = drop non-monotonic frames; cfr = renumber to constant 1/framerate intervals, duplicating frames into gaps and dropping frames that arrive too soon; drop = like vfr but also drops near-duplicates within half a frame duration of the previous emission.

audio_sync.description: Per-output audio resync compensation. Mirrors the legacy ffmpeg -async N flag (removed from the FFmpeg 8.0 CLI in favour of -af aresample=async=N). 0 = off; 1 = pad/trim the start so the first sample lands at PTS 0 (rendered as aresample=async=1:first_pts=0); N>1 = continuous soft compensation up to N samples/sec (rendered as aresample=async=N). Injected as an aresample filter node in front of the audio encoder.

shortest.description: Stop muxing as soon as the shortest input stream feeding this output ends. Mirrors ffmpeg -shortest (per-output scope, see fftools/ffmpeg_mux_init.c sync-queue setup). The runtime captures the PTS at which the first stream closes and stops emitting on every other stream of the same output.

max_file_size.description: Cap the encoded output at this many bytes. Mirrors ffmpeg -fs SIZE: before each WritePacket the runtime queries avio_tell on the muxer's IO context and stops with EOF (writing a clean trailer) once the limit is reached. 0 = unlimited.

muxdelay.description: Maximum demux-decode delay the muxer is allowed to buffer ahead, in seconds. Mirrors ffmpeg -muxdelay (per-output float, fftools/ffmpeg_mux_init.c L3447 — written as oc->max_delay = muxdelay * AV_TIME_BASE). FFmpeg default is 0.7s; 0 leaves the muxer default unchanged.

muxpreload.description: Initial demux-decode delay (pre-roll), in seconds. Mirrors ffmpeg -muxpreload (per-output float, fftools/ffmpeg_mux_init.c L3444 — emitted into the muxer AVDict as preload = muxpreload * AV_TIME_BASE). Most muxers ignore this; the historic consumer is libavformat/mpegenc.c. 0 leaves the default unchanged.

avoid_negative_ts.description: Libavformat's automatic timestamp shift policy at the muxer (AVFormatContext.avoid_negative_ts; libavformat/options_table.h L95-99). Mirrors ffmpeg -avoid_negative_ts. auto = shift only when the muxer requires it (FFmpeg default); disabled = pass through; make_non_negative = shift just enough to keep ts >= 0; make_zero = shift so the first ts is exactly 0. Required for clean MP4/MOV writes when input PTS are negative (typical with -ss + -copyts).

vn.description: Drop every video stream from this output's muxer. Mirrors ffmpeg -vn (OPT_OUTPUT half of fftools/ffmpeg_opt.c L1977). Filtered before expandImplicitEncoders so no implicit video encoder is created.

an.description: Drop every audio stream from this output's muxer. Mirrors ffmpeg -an (fftools/ffmpeg_opt.c L2078).

sn.description: Drop every subtitle stream from this output's muxer. Mirrors ffmpeg -sn (fftools/ffmpeg_opt.c L2115).

dn.description: Drop every data stream from this output's muxer. Mirrors ffmpeg -dn (fftools/ffmpeg_opt.c L2187).

realtime.description: Per-output Phase 7 pre-roll overrides. Unset fields fall back to global_options.prebuffer_*.

metadata.description: Container-level key/value metadata written into the output (`-metadata key=value`). Replaces any metadata mapped from inputs via `input.map_metadata`.

streams.description: Per-stream metadata + disposition overrides. Mirrors ffmpeg `-metadata:s:<type>:<idx> key=value` and `-disposition:s:<type>:<idx> flags`.

chapters.description: Explicit chapter table. Replaces any chapters mapped from inputs via `input.map_chapters`. Container must support chapters (matroska, mp4, ogg, ffmetadata, ...).

attachments.description: Files muxed into the container as AVMEDIA_TYPE_ATTACHMENT streams (matroska / mkv / webm only). Mirrors FFmpeg `-attach FILE`.

cover_art.description: Path to a cover-art image (JPEG / PNG / any libavformat-decodable format) embedded as an AVMEDIA_TYPE_VIDEO stream with AV_DISPOSITION_ATTACHED_PIC before avformat_write_header. Supported containers: mp4 / m4a / mov / ipod / mp3 / mkv / matroska. Mirrors `-i cover.jpg -map 1:v -c:v:1 copy -disposition:v:1 attached_pic`. Wave 11 #64.

kind.description: Output discriminator. '' / 'file' open a single muxer at `url`; 'tee' uses libavformat's tee muxer to fan one encoded stream out to N targets. When 'tee', `targets` is required and `url` / `format` are ignored.

targets.description: Slaves of a tee output (only valid when kind='tee'). Mirrors the FFmpeg `-f tee "[opt=val]url|[opt=val]url"` slaves grammar.

pass.description: Two-pass video encoding bit-field. 0 = single-pass (default); 1 = analysis pass (-pass 1, AV_CODEC_FLAG_PASS1); 2 = final pass (-pass 2, AV_CODEC_FLAG_PASS2); 3 = both bits set. The job is run twice by the caller: once with pass=1, once with pass=2, against the same passlogfile prefix. Honoured only on video encoders.

passlogfile.description: Per-stream statistics file prefix for two-pass video encoding. Rendered as `<prefix>-<stream-idx>.log`. Empty defaults to `ffmpeg2pass`. Honoured only when `pass != 0`. Mirrors FFmpeg `-passlogfile`.

loudnorm_pass.description: Two-pass EBU R128 loudnorm shuttle. 0 = single-pass (default); 1 = analysis (libavfilter/af_loudnorm.c writes input_i / input_tp / input_lra / input_thresh / target_offset to a JSON stats file via print_format=json + stats_file); 2 = apply (the runtime reads pass-1 stats and injects measured_I / measured_TP / measured_LRA / measured_thresh / offset into the same loudnorm node). The job is run twice by the caller against the same loudnorm_statsfile prefix. FFmpeg has no flag for this — every documented two-pass loudnorm recipe wires it by hand via stderr-scraping.

loudnorm_statsfile.description: Prefix for the per-loudnorm-node JSON stats file rendered as `<prefix>-<idx>.json` where `<idx>` is the per-run loudnorm-node ordinal. Empty defaults to `mm-loudnorm`. Honoured only when `loudnorm_pass != 0`.

force_key_frames.description: FFmpeg `-force_key_frames` spec. Three grammars: `expr:EXPR` (libavutil expression evaluated per video frame; vars n / n_forced / prev_forced_n / prev_forced_t / t — canonical idiom `expr:gte(t,n_forced*2)` for a 2 s GOP); `source` (copy keyframes from source); or comma-separated time list (`3.0,7.5,10.25`, float seconds). Required for HLS / DASH segmenters. Honoured only on video encoders.

hls.description: Typed HLS muxer options. Only valid when `format="hls"`. Promoted from the generic `options` AVDict bag; on key collision the typed field wins. Mirrors `libavformat/hlsenc.c`.

dash.description: Typed DASH muxer options. Only valid when `format="dash"`. Promoted from the generic `options` AVDict bag; on key collision the typed field wins. Mirrors `libavformat/dashenc.c`.

color.description: Per-stream color metadata (range, primaries, transfer, matrix, chroma_location). Names are the canonical libavutil av_color_*_name values; unknown names are rejected at validate time. Mirrors ffmpeg -color_range / -color_primaries / -color_trc / -colorspace / -chroma_sample_location.

hdr.description: SMPTE ST 2086 mastering display + CTA-861.3 content light level metadata. Requires an HDR-capable codec (hevc/av1/vp9 or copy) and container (mp4/mov/matroska/webm/mpegts), and color.transfer ∈ {smpte2084, arib-std-b67} when color is also set.

sar.description: Output sample aspect ratio (`setsar` shorthand / FFmpeg per-encoder SAR). Forms: `A:B`, `A/B`, or decimal. Mutually exclusive with `dar`. Empty leaves the encoder default unchanged.

dar.description: Output display aspect ratio (`setdar` shorthand / FFmpeg `-aspect`). Forms: `A:B`, `A/B`, or decimal. Resolved to SAR using the encoder's width/height: SAR = (DAR_num*H)/(DAR_den*W). Mutually exclusive with `sar`.

encoder_time_base.description: Encoder AVCodecContext.time_base override (mirrors per-stream `-enc_time_base`). Accepts `"demux"`, `"filter"`, or `"N/D"` rational. Subtitle outputs reject the field at validate time.

field_order.description: Encoder AVCodecContext.field_order (libavcodec/defs.h::AVFieldOrder). Empty leaves AV_FIELD_UNKNOWN. Mirrors `-field_order`.

interlaced_encode.description: Toggles AV_CODEC_FLAG_INTERLACED_DCT|INTERLACED_ME on the encoder context (avcodec.h L310/L331). Required for broadcast 1080i workflows. Incompatible with `field_order=progressive`.

segment_on_metadata.description: Metadata.Custom key that triggers a segment cut at the next video keyframe. The output URL must contain a printf integer verb (e.g. %05d) for segment numbering. Incompatible with kind=tee, realtime pre-roll, and cover_art.

segment_format.description: Muxer format name override for each segment file when segment_on_metadata is set. Defaults to format or the URL extension.

select.description: Comma-separated FFmpeg stream specifiers (`v`, `a:0`, ...). Empty = all streams.

use_fifo.description: Wrap slave in libavformat's fifo muxer for buffered async writes.

fifo_options.description: `;`-separated key=value forwarded to the fifo muxer when use_fifo is set.

options.description: Free-form additional `[opt=val]` pairs for obscure tee-slave AVOptions.

path.description: Filesystem path of the file to embed.

filename.description: Stream `filename` metadata. Defaults to basename of `path`.

threads.description: Default codec thread count for all decoders/encoders. 0 = FFmpeg auto-detect. Can be overridden per node via params.threads.

thread_type.description: Default codec threading model. Can be overridden per node via params.thread_type.

highest_quality_preset.description: Phase 6 adaptive stepping: the slowest (highest quality) preset the controller is allowed to use. The controller may step freely to any faster preset to maintain real-time throughput. Omit to allow stepping all the way to the initial encoder preset.

preset_group_step.description: When true (default), step every eligible video encoder together once a quorum is simultaneously behind.

target_fps.description: Graph-level real-time fps target; 0 = derive from per-node fps_target.

encoder_input_buffer_frames.description: Per-encoder input channel capacity in frames; 0 = pipeline default. 96 (~4 s @ 24 fps) is recommended in realtime mode to absorb a preset close+reopen without stalling upstream filters.

prebuffer_duration_seconds.description: Phase 7 per-output preroll fill target (seconds) before the muxer is allowed to write. Default 4.0 s (1.0 s for audio-only outputs). 0 disables prerolling.

prebuffer_max_seconds.description: Phase 7 hard cap on the preroll buffer; once exceeded the oldest packet is dropped. Defaults to 2 × prebuffer_duration_seconds.

read_rate.description: Global default demuxer read-rate applied to every input whose own read_rate is unset. Paces packet reads to (read_rate × realtime); 0 = unpaced; 1.0 mirrors ffmpeg -re. Set to 1.0 with realtime:true for live-restream jobs.

read_rate_initial_burst.description: Global default for per-input read_rate_initial_burst. Seconds of unpaced burst allowed before the read-rate pacer engages. Defaults to 0.5 (FFmpeg default) when read_rate is non-zero and this field is unset.

read_rate_catchup.description: Global default for per-input read_rate_catchup. Multiplier used when the demuxer falls behind real-time; must be >= read_rate. Defaults to read_rate × 1.05 when unset.

realtime_log_path.description: When non-empty and realtime:true, the adaptive controller writes one JSONL record per observation tick (every 500 ms) to this file. Each record contains the full per-node performance snapshot, controller cool-down counters, and any decisions made that tick. Use for post-hoc diagnosis of realtime performance anomalies. The file is created (or truncated) when the pipeline starts. Example: "/tmp/rt_debug.jsonl".

rpu_present.description: RPU NAL units present in bitstream (default true).

list_size.description: Maximum entries kept in the playlist (`hls_list_size`); 0 keeps all.

segment_type.description: `hls_segment_type`. `fmp4` switches to CMAF-style fragmented MP4 segments.

start_number.description: First sequence number in the playlist (`start_number`).

master_pl_name.description: Master playlist filename (`master_pl_name`); required for ABR ladders.

var_stream_map.description: Variant-stream mapping (`var_stream_map`), e.g. `"v:0,a:0 v:1,a:0"`. Requires master_pl_name.

flags.description: List of `hls_flags` token names; joined with `+` before being passed to libavformat.

window_size.description: Maximum segments kept in the manifest (`window_size`); 0 keeps all.

extra_window_size.description: Extra segments retained on disk past `window_size` (`extra_window_size`).

hls_playlist.description: Also emit HLS .m3u8 playlists alongside the DASH manifest (`hls_playlist`); the CMAF dual-pack mode.

flags.description: List of `dash_flags` token names; joined with `+` before being passed to libavformat.

path.description: Filesystem path of the asset (absolute or relative to working directory / MEDIAMOLDER_ASSET_PATH).

kind.description: Asset type for GUI classification: font (TTF/OTF), model (ML model file), lut (.cube/.3dl), other.

desc.description: Optional human-readable label shown in the GUI asset registry.

name.description: Symbolic label for the device (e.g. "gpu0"). Referenced from NodeDef.device.

type.description: Hardware device type: "cuda", "vaapi", "qsv", "videotoolbox", etc. Passed to av_hwdevice_find_type_by_name.

device.description: OS-level device specifier (e.g. "/dev/dri/renderD128", "0"). Empty string selects the first available device.

options.description: Extra AVDictionary entries forwarded to av_hwdevice_ctx_create.
